results.title: Inductors
results.creator_credit: John Durst, Frank A. Hoare and Merton Park
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Electrical Induction and Capacity”.
A soft iron core introduced into a coil carrying an alternating current shows what is meant by self-induction. Diagrams explain the working of choke and illustrate the effects of varying frequency. The effect of an inductor on the make and break of a circuit and its application in the starting of a fluorescent lamp is shown. Illustrates applications of inductors in bells, fluorescent tubes, signals and other electrical equipment.
results.creator_credit_for_label: John Durst, Frank A. Hoare and Merton Park

results.title: Effects on alternating current and power factor
results.creator_credit: John Durst, Frank A. Hoare and Merton Park
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Electrical Induction and Capacity”.
Illustrates the effects of inductors and capacitors on alternating and direct current circuits; reduction of power factor by high natural capacity or inductance of AC circuit; use of inductors or capacitors to improve power-factors; applications to fluorescent lamps and telephone cables.

results.title: Capacitors
results.creator_credit: John Durst, Frank A. Hoare and Merton Park
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Electrical Induction and Capacity”.
A brief summary of facts on electrostatics is followed by a demonstration of experiments with the Leyden jar; explanation of phenomenon in terms of build-up of charge; electroscope measurements when two charged plates are moved in relation to one another and when the dielectric is changed; capacitor or condensor factors affecting capacity; examples of capacitors at work.

results.title: Controlling absenteeism
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Effective Supervisor Series”.
Focuses on the causes of absenteeism in business and offers practical solutions. Identifies five categories of absentees and provides supervisors with coping strategies and tactics to avoid in dealing with staff.

results.title: Decreasing inappropriate behaviours (TEFC): Overcorrection
results.creator_credit: John Socha and Macomb Community College Instructional Television
results.brief_description:
Episode number 7 of Series “Effective Behavioural Programming”.
Tape 7 : Overcorrection is a punishment procedure in which the misbehaving student is required to overcorrect the environmental effects of his misbehaviour (restitution) and to practice appropriate forms of behaviour in those situations where the misbehaviour commonly occurs (positive practice). NB. This videotape is on 525 line NTSC.

results.title: Decreasing inappropriate behaviours (TEFC): physical restraint
results.creator_credit: John Socha and Macomb Community College Instructional Television
results.brief_description:
Episode number 5 of Series “Effective Behavioural Programming”.
Tape 5 : Discusses physical restraint and punishment programs. Teachers must be cautious in designing and implementing them because of their aversive nature. Both physical restraint and punishment have very powerful advantages, but they also have some serious drawbacks NB. This videotape is on 525 line NTSC

results.title: Decreasing inappropriate behaviours (TEFC): timeout
results.creator_credit: John Sucho and Macomb Community College Instructional Television
results.brief_description:
Episode number 4 of Series “Effective Behavioural Programming”.
Tape 4 : Timeout is a procedure in which positive reinforcement is withdrawn for a prespecified period of time following the performance of a misbehaviour. There are two types of timeout: exclusionary and non-exclusionary timeout. NB. This tape plays on 525 line NTSC
results.creator_credit_for_label: John Sucho and Macomb Community College Instructional Television
